channel.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India
channel.description: World News Today: Read the latest world news headlines, Current International breaking news world wide. In depth analysis and top news headlines world wide.
channel.copyright: Copyright:(C) Tue, 07 Jul 2026 18:51:34 IST Bennett Coleman & Co. Ltd, http://in.indiatimes.com/policyterms/1554651.cms

image.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India

item.title: Saudi Arabia simplifies travel with all-in-one package visa including flights, hotels and e-visa services
item.description: Saudi Arabia has launched a pilot Tourist Package Visa service for easier travel. This new initiative combines flights, hotels, and visa processing into one booking. The program aims to simplify travel arrangements for visitors planning trips to the Kingdom. Currently, the pilot phase is available for travelers from select countries like India and Pakistan. This integrated approach supports Saudi Arabia's ambitious Vision 2030 tourism goals.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Interpol-wanted woman in Monaco bombing case found shot dead near Kyiv; two detained
item.description: Anastasiia Berezovska, a Ukrainian woman linked to a bomb attack in Monaco, was discovered dead near Kyiv, having been shot. She was under an Interpol warrant for attempted murder. Authorities have detained two suspects, one of whom is an intelligence officer, in connection with her murder. The bombing had targeted a prosperous businessman from Ukraine living in Monaco, leaving him and his family injured.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Quote of the day by Saddam Hussein: 'A lion doesn't care if a monkey in a tree is laughing at him'
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: World's first diamond to travel to space will launch aboard India's Vikram-1 rocket
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: Why does Alaska go 65 days without sunrise: The science behind polar night
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: Cockroaches can survive without their heads for a week: The science behind one of nature's toughest insects
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: 5-month-old startup wants to launch 100,000 AI data centre satellites into space
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: What will happen on 13 April 2029: A giant asteroid will pass closer than some satellites and be visible to billions
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: A Japan mountain trail reopened after a fatal bear attack. Hours later, another brown bear stranded four hikers
item.description: Four hikers were stranded on a Hokkaido mountain after encountering a brown bear. They waited for over three hours before rescue teams arrived by helicopter. The bear had blocked the trail, preventing the group from continuing their descent. This incident occurred on Mount Rausu, which had recently reopened after a fatal attack. Bear encounters are increasing across Japan, prompting official warnings.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Indian hairstylist charged with sexual abuse on a cruise docked in Seattle, offered free massages to women, then molested passengers
item.description: An Indian hairstylist, Pranit Pawar, is facing serious allegations of abusive sexual contact aboard a cruise ship. Accused of luring passengers into false massage offers, he allegedly proceeded to inappropriately touch victims in a confined space. The FBI has launched an investigation into the troubling incidents, which took place under US maritime jurisdiction. Pawar has confessed that his actions fell well outside his professional duties.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: America's 250-year time capsule has been buried: Here's what people in 2276 will discover
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Watch: Ukraine targets Russia's 'shadow fleet'; hits 8 fuel tankers on Crimea route
item.description: Ukraine’s forces executed drone strikes that targeted eight Russian fuel tankers during the night. These tankers were supposedly supplying fuel to Crimea in the Sea of Azov. The operation aimed to impede Russian military supply routes and logistics. This move is a continuation of a series of Ukrainian assaults on Crimea's infrastructure, which has led to significant fuel shortages on the peninsula.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: China battered by extreme weather: Floods, tornadoes & landslides kills over 20, videos capture destruction
item.description: Torrential rain and tornadoes killed at least twenty people across China. Over 330 others sustained injuries from extreme weather events in several provinces. A landslide in Gansu Province buried thirty-three people, with twelve still missing. Record rainfall triggered severe flooding in Guangxi, affecting over 93,000 residents. President Xi Jinping ordered all-out efforts for rescue and disaster relief operations.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Florida dad grabbed a 202-pound Burmese python, then it started dragging him towards a canal before his entire family took down the giant snake
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 'Confrontational statements' on Russia: Kremlin slams Nato's Ankara summit
item.description: Russia continues working-level discussions with the United States regarding Ukraine peace efforts. Kremlin spokespersons expressed hope for successful US diplomatic initiatives to achieve peace. Moscow will closely observe the outcomes of the NATO summit in Ankara. Russia noted confrontational statements made by NATO members before the meeting. The ongoing conflict in Ukraine sees fighting along multiple fronts.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: NASA Chief says China can soon outsmart US in space exploration: But could shape the future of space
item.description: In an exciting twist of cosmic competition, China and the United States have sparked a modern space race aimed at lunar exploration. With NASA's Artemis program facing delays, China is quickly advancing its goals. This fierce rivalry focuses on securing lunar resources and establishing a lasting foothold on the Moon, potentially paving the way for humanity's next giant leap towards Mars.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: 'Stop the genocide': Baloch protesters in Germany raise alarm over Pakistan atrocities
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: UAE revokes Ajman's Horizon University licence over 'severe and repeated' violations; students to be transferred
item.description: The UAE Ministry of Higher Education revoked Horizon University College's license due to severe and repeated violations. This action immediately halts all academic activities and student admissions at the institution. The ministry is coordinating with other universities to ensure students can continue their education. Affected learners will receive support during this transition process. This move reinforces the UAE's commitment to maintaining high academic standards.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 'My visit continues': French President Macron after close shave in Syria amid explosions near hotel
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron continued his Syria visit after explosions near his hotel. Two blasts struck central Damascus, wounding at least eighteen people earlier on Tuesday. Macron was unharmed and proceeded with his scheduled meeting with Syrian President Ahmed al-Sharaa. Further details indicated two explosive devices detonated near the Four Seasons Hotel. No group has claimed responsibility for the recent attack in Damascus.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: A Native American tribe once relied on oil and gas; now its desert land will help power Meta’s new data center
item.description: The Ute Mountain Ute Tribe is developing a large solar project. This initiative will supply clean energy to a new Meta data center. The project marks a significant shift from the tribe's oil and gas revenue. Renewable energy developers face policy uncertainties and permitting challenges. The tribe secured approvals before new federal regulations took effect.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk
